qa_pairs.question: What should users be aware of when moving between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users need to be aware that while clusters have a relatively high degree of uniformity, particularly general purpose clusters, there are still significant distinctions between them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended tool for transferring large amounts of data between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: To transfer data from one cluster to another, we recommend using Globus, especially when the amount of data involved exceeds a few hundred megabytes.

qa_pairs.question: How can I access each cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Each cluster is accessible via SSH.

qa_pairs.question: Are my username and password consistent across all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, your username and password are the same across all of the clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any special steps required to access the Niagara cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, accessing Niagara does require a further step.

qa_pairs.question: Do the general purpose clusters have a similar filesystem structure?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each of the general purpose clusters has a similar filesystem structure.

qa_pairs.question: Is data mirrored between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no mirroring of data between the clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Are the contents of my home, scratch, and project spaces shared across clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the contents of your home, scratch, and project spaces are independent on each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Can filesystem quota policies differ between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the quota policies may differ between clusters, though generally not by much.

qa_pairs.question: If my group has a special storage allocation on one cluster, will it be available on other clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, a special storage allocation, such as `$HOME/projects/rrg-jsmith`, will normally only be available on the particular cluster where it was allocated.

qa_pairs.question: If my group's default project space quota was increased on one cluster, does this change apply to other clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if your group requested that the default project space quota on a cluster be increased, this change will only have been made on that specific cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Are globally installed modules consistent across all general purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the collection of globally installed modules is the same across all of our general purpose clusters, distributed using CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to re-install Python virtual environments or R and Perl packages when moving to a new cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, any Python virtual environments or R and Perl packages you've installed in your directories on one cluster will need to be re-installed on the new cluster.

qa_pairs.question: If I customized my `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one cluster, do I need to re-do it on a new cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you modified your `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one cluster to customize your environment, you will need to modify the same file on the new cluster you're using.

qa_pairs.question: If I installed a program in my directories on one cluster, will it be accessible on another cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if you installed a particular program in your directories, it will need to be re-installed on the new cluster because filesystems are independent between clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What system do all clusters use for job submission?
qa_pairs.answer: All of our clusters use Slurm for job submission.

qa_pairs.question: Will all parts of a job submission script work across different clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Many parts of a job submission script will work across clusters, but some aspects like CPU cores per node/GPU or memory per node/core may vary and require adaptation.

qa_pairs.question: Do the number of CPU cores per node or per GPU vary between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the number of CPU cores per node or per GPU may significantly vary, so you should check the page of the cluster you are using.

qa_pairs.question: Does the amount of memory per node or per core change between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the amount of memory per node or per core also varies, so you may need to adapt your job submission script to account for this.

qa_pairs.question: Do all compute nodes on every cluster have direct Internet access?
qa_pairs.answer: No, on some clusters, compute nodes may not have direct Internet access. You should access the cluster's link to find out about site-specific policies.

qa_pairs.question: Does each research group have access to a default allocation on every cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each research group has access to a default allocation on every cluster, for example, `#SBATCH --account=def-jsmith`.

qa_pairs.question: Are special compute allocations like RRG or contributed allocations available on all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, special compute allocations like RRG or contributed allocations are tied to a particular cluster and will not be available on other clusters.
